Class LootManager

java.lang.Object
net.sylphian.minecraft.fishing.loot.LootManager
public class LootManager extends Object
Manages the loot rolling logic for the fishing system.

Rather than rolling a rarity first and then searching for fish, the manager builds an eligible pool upfront from all fish that match the current catch context. Rarity is then rolled and applied as a filter against that pool, ensuring biome, height, and time restrictions are always respected.

Roll Process

  1. Collect all fish matching the current biome, Y coordinate, and time of day
  2. Roll a rarity using base chances modified by weather multipliers
  3. Filter the eligible pool to fish of the rolled rarity
  4. Pick a fish using weighted random selection
  5. If no fish of the rolled rarity exist in the eligible pool, fall back to a weighted pick across the entire eligible pool respecting rarity chances

Weather Modifiers

Each weather condition (WeatherCondition) applies a multiplier to each rarity's base chance. Multipliers are loaded from config.yml and clamped to a maximum of 1.0.

See Also:

  • Rarity
  • WeatherCondition

    • LootManager

      public LootManager(List<FishEntry> allFish, ConfigLoader config)
      Constructs a new LootManager.
      Parameters:
      allFish - the list of all available fish entries
      config - the configuration loader for retrieving multipliers

  • Method Details

    • rollCatch

      public CatchResult rollCatch(org.bukkit.block.Biome biome, WeatherCondition weather, double hookY, long worldTime)
      Rolls a catch result for the given context. First builds an eligible pool of all fish matching the biome, Y coordinate, and time of day. Then rolls a rarity applying weather multipliers, and picks a fish from the matching pool. If no fish of the rolled rarity exist in the eligible pool, falls back to a weighted pick across the entire eligible pool.
      Parameters:
      biome - the biome where the fishing hook landed
      weather - the current weather condition in the world
      hookY - the Y coordinate of the fishing hook
      worldTime - the current world time in ticks (0-24000)
      Returns:
      a CatchResult containing the fish ID, rarity, weight, and built ItemStack
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if no fish are configured for the given context
